title: ON VEHICLE SERVICE — Brush Holder

figures_detected.description: On-vehicle photo showing brush holder location with arrow indicating position
Illustration of brush holder showing brush limit mark position
Illustration showing incorrect practice — bending brush — marked with X

steps.text: Remove two bolts securing brush holder.
Move brush holder slightly to the right and raise to remove it.

content_blocks.text: Note: Do not bend the brush when removing it from the brush holder.
Inspect the brush wear distance. Replace the brush if the brush end gets close to the brush limit mark position.
Note: When determining whether to replace or continue use of a brush, consider if the brush will last until the next inspection of the brush. Observe the following points when replacing the brush.
If the contact area of the brush is scratched, inspect the slip ring because foreign matter may become attached to it. (Refer to Removal and Installation for slip ring cleaning procedure.)
Check the continuity between the harness and the terminal (5 mm screw) and the insulation between the harness and bracket.
Before separating the engine and transaxle, remove the brush holder from the case to prevent damage.
Be careful not to bend or damage the brush when removing the brush holder from the case.
Do not move the brush up and down unnecessarily with fingertip. This may cause the lead wire to disconnect.
When there is oil on the brush, check for oil leaks and repair as necessary. Replace the brush holder and electromagnetic clutch if required.

cross_references: Removal and Installation for slip ring cleaning procedure
